architecture.summary: Sign-Aware Gated Sparse Autoencoder (SA-GSAE) extends SAEs by replacing single-sided sparsity with two-sided gating over signed magnitudes. A polarity-sensitive gate selects support on either positive or negative sign; a signed-magnitude reconstruction path bypasses L1 shrinkage; an auxiliary reconstruction loss prevents gate collapse. Bipolar latents encode both signs along a shared direction via a Bi-Jump-ReLU activation, allowing anticorrelated features to share a single latent when beneficial. The model is trained end-to-end on LLM hidden activations with joint optimization of gating, magnitude, and auxiliary paths.

biblio.title: Sign-Aware Gated Sparse Autoencoders: Modeling Anticorrelated Features with Bi-Jump-ReLU Activations

claims.statement: Half-width SA-GSAE at width H strictly Pareto-dominates full-width Gated SAE at 2H over the entire swept L0 overlap on 3 of 6 cells (both MLP-output hookpoints and resid-mid/Pythia-1B)

claims.dataset: Pythia-1B and SmolLM3-3B LLM activations (6 cells, 3 seeds)
claims.statement: On remaining 3 cells SA-GSAE matches R² within 0.025 (max gap -0.008) while cutting dead fraction by 0.35–0.62 absolute
Sweep-geomean dead-fraction reductions are ~100x–500x on MLP-output cells and Pythia-1B resid, ~2x–4x on attention cells and SmolLM3-3B resid
Ablation: removing auxiliary loss causes reconstruction to collapse to R² 0.27 with 98% dead latents
Ablation: tying r_i^+ = r_i^- (symmetric variant) is indistinguishable from separate paths (|ΔR²| = 0.0015)
Two-sided gate and auxiliary loss are load-bearing components
On MLP-output hookpoints, most latents carry both polarities; on attention hookpoints, bipolar structure concentrates in a small set of top latents
Full-width SA-GSAE exhibits a reproducible reconstruction collapse at SmolLM3-3B resid that the half-width variant entirely avoids
